description: Synthetic medical QA cases driving the simulated LLM in benchmarks.hallucination_rate against MedicalDomain. Same tag semantics as synthetic_qa.json; the simulator picks a PubMed URL for valid sources so MedicalDomain's allow-list passes on successful attempts. Run with: python -m benchmarks.hallucination_rate --domain medical --dataset benchmarks/datasets/medical_qa.json

tags.[EASY]: Confident, well-cited medical claim on the first attempt.
tags.[LOW_CONF]: Low-confidence answer — FactCheckGate rejects on confidence < 0.95.
tags.[BAD_SOURCE]: High confidence but cited host is outside the allow-list — FactCheckGate rejects.
tags.[CRITIC_FAIL]: High confidence with a valid source but Critic flags contradictory clinical guidance.
tags.[RECOVERS]: Low confidence on attempt 1, confident on attempt 2 — success after retry.

cases.query: [EASY] Is regular hand-washing associated with reduced gastrointestinal infection rates?
[EASY] Does smoking increase the risk of lung cancer?
[LOW_CONF] Is there definitive evidence linking moderate coffee intake to longevity?
[LOW_CONF] What is the exact 10-year survival rate for stage II melanoma in a 47-year-old?
[BAD_SOURCE] Cite the recommended pediatric dose of paracetamol.
[CRITIC_FAIL] Give a definitive but internally contradictory dosing recommendation.
[RECOVERS] Hedge first, then commit on retry: efficacy of routine vitamin D supplementation in healthy adults.
